name: Zoot Sims
abstract: John Haley "Zoot" Sims (October 29, 1925 – March 23, 1985) was an American jazz saxophonist, playing mainly tenor but also alto (and, later, soprano) saxophone. He first gained attention in the "Four Brothers" sax section of Woody Herman's big band, afterward enjoying a long solo career, often in partnership with fellow saxmen Gerry Mull…

about_quotes.speaker: Greg Thomas
about_quotes.evidence: I loved Zoot Sims. And that was like a shield that protected me against falling prey to that kind of thinking, man.
about_quotes.doc_title: Greg Thomas interviewed by Monk Rowe, Clinton, New York, October 11, 2014

about_quotes.speaker: Bill Holman
about_quotes.evidence: he was one of my dear friends after we were on Kenton’s band together... finally get to work with my old pal.
about_quotes.doc_title: Bill Holman interviewed by Monk Rowe, Los Angeles, California, February 13, 1999

about_quotes.speaker: Carmen Leggio
about_quotes.evidence: In the group was Zoot Sims, myself, Joe Newman, Billy Taylor, Kai Winding was the leader...
about_quotes.doc_title: Carmen Leggio interviewed by Monk Rowe, Tarrytown, New York, October 31, 1998

about_quotes.speaker: Candoli
about_quotes.evidence: The personnel was unbelievable... he got Lee Konitz, saxophone, Zoot Sims. That’s the ‘53 band... That was my favorite Kenton band.

about_quotes.speaker: Tony Marino
about_quotes.evidence: And I got to play with Zoot with Al. It was one of Zoot’s last gigs actually, at the Blue Note and I was thrilled to be part of it
about_quotes.doc_title: Tony Marino interviewed by Monk Rowe, via Zoom, August 25, 2025

about_quotes.speaker: Holman Bill
about_quotes.evidence: I really had a good time with Zoot. We went to Europe. ... Stan and I got to be good friends. ... It just was no fun without him.
about_quotes.doc_title: Holman Bill — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History
